publications.title: RGC: A Radio AGN Classifier Based on Deep Learning. I. A Semi-Supervised Model for the VLA Images of Bent Radio AGNs
publications.authors: M. S. Hossain, M. S. H. Shahal, A. Khan, K. M. B. Asad, P. Saikia, M. O. B. Jihad, D. P. Guha, F. Akter, S. Sen, A. A. Ali, M. A. Amin, A. Momen, A. K. M. M. Rahman
publications.abstract: Wide-angle tail (WAT) and narrow-angle tail (NAT) radio active galactic nuclei (RAGNs) are key tracers of dense environments in galaxy groups and clusters, yet no machine-learning classifier of bent RAGNs has been trained using both unlabeled data and purely visually inspected labels. We release the RGC Python package, which includes two newly preprocessed labeled datasets of 639 WATs and NATs derived from a publicly available catalog of visually inspected sources, along with a semi-supervised RGC model that leverages 20,000 unlabeled RAGNs. The RGC model integrates the self-supervised framework BYOL with E2CNN to form a semi-supervised binary classifier, attaining an accuracy of 88.88% with F1-scores of 0.90 for WATs and 0.85 for NATs.
publications.contribution: Joined the project near its completion; currently addressing peer reviewer comments from the journal committee and assisting in dataset re-preparation for resubmission

publications.title: PIE-ADA: Physics-Informed Ensemble with Adaptive Data Augmentation for Photometric Transient Classification
publications.venue: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence & Networking (QPAIN 2026)
publications.abstract: The upcoming LSST is expected to observe approximately 10 million astronomical transient events per night, creating an urgent need for automated classification. PIE-ADA generates physically realistic synthetic light curves using astrophysically motivated transformations: correlated noise injection, cosmological time dilation, wavelength-dependent dust extinction, and observation phase shifting. Evaluated on the PLAsTiCC dataset across 14 classes, LightGBM achieved a weighted log loss of 0.5763 (±0.0083) and 80.33% accuracy, improving over baselines by 24–49%. The full pipeline completes in under 37 minutes with per-object classification in less than 0.05 seconds.

publications.title: LunaXNet: A Novel Pre-Post-AI Framework for Mineralogical Segmentation, Quantification, and Interpretation from Lunar Hyperspectral Data
publications.authors: D. P. Guha, M. A. Y. Srizon, et al.
publications.venue: Target Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
publications.abstract: We introduce LunaXNet, an end-to-end deep learning framework for automated lunar mineralogy mapping from Chandrayaan-1 M3 hyperspectral data. The framework integrates a Pre-AI preprocessing pipeline, the LunaXNet hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture for semantic segmentation of mineral species (olivine, pyroxene, plagioclase, ilmenite, spinel) at sub-kilometer resolution, and a Post-AI interpretation module. LunaXNet achieves 92%+ pixel-wise accuracy, outperforming traditional spectral unmixing and existing CNN baselines. Grad-CAM visualization confirms the model learns physically meaningful spectral features.
publications.status_note: Model architecture finalized; experiments 60% complete; manuscript in early draft stage

publications.title: RGC v2.0: Hierarchical Multi-Class Classification of Radio AGN Morphologies
publications.authors: M. S. H. Shahal, A. Khan, D. P. Guha, K. M. B. Asad, et al.
publications.abstract: We present RGC v2.0, an extended version of the Radha Gobinda Chandra radio galaxy classifier, featuring hierarchical multi-class classification of radio AGN morphologies including FRI, FRII, WAT, NAT, Compact, and Hybrid types. The framework processes images from LOFAR/LoTSS (144 MHz) and VLA/FIRST (1.4 GHz) surveys using semi-supervised CNN architecture.
publications.contribution: Semi-supervised learning implementation, bent morphology classification (WAT/NAT), data preprocessing pipeline development, model training and validation

publications.title: Vision-Language Models for Multi-Wavelength Galaxy Characterization
publications.authors: M. S. Hossain, et al., D. P. Guha, K. M. B. Asad, et al.
publications.venue: Target Journal: The Astrophysical Journal Supplement Series
publications.abstract: We present a multimodal AI system leveraging Vision-Language Models for automated characterization of galaxies across multiple wavelengths. The system ingests galaxy images from optical (Pan-STARRS, SDSS), infrared (WISE), radio (LOFAR, VLA), and X-ray surveys to generate comprehensive astrophysical descriptions and classifications.
publications.contribution: Vision LLM pipeline development, multi-wavelength data integration, automated description generation system

publications.title: GAN-Enhanced Gastrointestinal Disease Classification with Dual-Stage Preprocessing and Interpretable Post-Processing Framework
publications.authors: T. Sabira, D. P. Guha, M. A. Y. Srizon, et al.
publications.abstract: We propose a comprehensive pipeline integrating dual-stage preprocessing, StyleGAN2-ADA based data augmentation, a multi-architecture ensemble (EfficientNetV2, ResNet152, ViT), and interpretable post-processing via Grad-CAM and SHAP. Our framework achieves 96.2% accuracy across 8 gastrointestinal disease classes. Grad-CAM heatmaps align with expert-identified pathological features in 91% of cases.
publications.contribution: GAN implementation and training, synthetic data quality assessment, ensemble model integration, Grad-CAM visualization, literature review, manuscript co-writing

publications.title: Physics-Inspired Multimodal Emotion Recognition for Telemedicine Triage
publications.abstract: We present a physics-inspired multimodal emotion recognition framework for telemedicine triage using the MELD dataset, mapping emotions to clinical risk levels. Key contributions include the Telehealth Acoustic Augmentation Bank (TAAB) and a physics-aware loss function incorporating temporal and spectral smoothness constraints. The fusion model (BERT + TAAB + physics-loss CNN) achieves superior performance on high-risk emotion detection.
publications.contribution: TAAB design and implementation, physics-aware loss function formulation, ablation studies, related work on physics-informed neural networks and audio augmentation, discussion section co-writing
